sourceQuestion:
The difference between the sum of the first 2n natural numbers and the sum of the first n odd natural numbers is ______.

A. n^2 = n
B. n^2 + n
C. 2n^2 = n
D. 2n^2 + n

parsed.stem: The difference between the sum of the first 2n natural numbers and the sum of the first n odd natural numbers is ______.

sourceQuestion:
Insert seven numbers between 2 and 34, such that the resulting sequence including 2 and 34 is an arithmetic progression. The sum of these inserted seven numbers is ______.

A. 120
B. 126
C. 126
D. 130

parsed.stem: Insert seven numbers between 2 and 34, such that the resulting sequence including 2 and 34 is an arithmetic progression. The sum of these inserted seven numbers is ______.

sourceQuestion:
In a locality, the houses are numbered in the following way: The house numbers on one side of a road are consecutive odd integers starting from 301, while the house numbers on the other side of the road are consecutive even numbers starting from 302. The total number of houses is the same on both sides of the road.

If the difference of the sum of house numbers between the two sides of the road is 27, then the number of houses on each side of the road is:

A. 27
B. 52
C. 54
D. 26

parsed.stem:
In a locality, the houses are numbered in the following way: The house numbers on one side of a road are consecutive odd integers starting from 301, while the house numbers on the other side of the road are consecutive even numbers starting from 302. The total number of houses is the same on both sides of the road.

If the difference of the sum of house numbers between the two sides of the road is 27, then the number of houses on each side of the road is:

sourceQuestion:
If the sum of the first 20 consecutive positive odd numbers is divided by 20^2, the result is:

A. 1
B. 20
C. 2
D. 1/2

parsed.stem: If the sum of the first 20 consecutive positive odd numbers is divided by 20^2, the result is:

The sum of n terms of the series 4 + 44 + 444 + ... is:

sourceQuestion:
How many integers are there between 100 and 1000 all of whose digits are even?

A. 60
B. 80
C. 100
D. 90

parsed.stem: How many integers are there between 100 and 1000 all of whose digits are even?

sourceQuestion:
The average marks obtained by a class in an examination were calculated as 30.5. However, while checking the marks entered, the teacher found that the marks of one student were entered incorrectly as 42 instead of 24. After correcting the marks, the average becomes 30. How many students does the class have?

A. 29
B. 28
C. 36
D. 32

parsed.stem: The average marks obtained by a class in an examination were calculated as 30.5. However, while checking the marks entered, the teacher found that the marks of one student were entered incorrectly as 42 instead of 24. After correcting the marks, the average becomes 30. How many students does the class have?

sourceQuestion:
The average of the monthly salaries of M, N and S is 4000. The average of the monthly salaries of N, S and P is 5000. The monthly salary of P is 6000. What is the monthly salary of P as a percentage of the monthly salary of M?

A. 50%
B. 75%
C. 100%
D. 125%

parsed.stem: The average of the monthly salaries of M, N and S is 4000. The average of the monthly salaries of N, S and P is 5000. The monthly salary of P is 6000. What is the monthly salary of P as a percentage of the monthly salary of M?

sourceQuestion:
For the past m days, the average daily production at a company was 100 units per day. If today's production of 180 units changes the average to 110 units per day, what is the value of m?

A. 16  B. 10  C. 7  D. 5

parsed.stem: For the past m days, the average daily production at a company was 100 units per day. If today's production of 180 units changes the average to 110 units per day, what is the value of m?

sourceQuestion:
The chart below shows the number of cars bought by Millennials and Gen X buyers in a country from the year 2010 to 2020, as well as the yearly fuel consumption of the country (in Million liters). Considering the data presented in the chart, which one of the following options is true?

A. The percentage increase in fuel consumption from 2010 to 2020 is more than the percentage increase in fuel consumption from 2015 to 2020.
B. The increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is less than the decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020.
C. The decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is more than the decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2010 to 2015.
D. The decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is more than the increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from 2015 to 2020.
sourceAnswer: C. The decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is more than the decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2010 to 2015.

parsed.stem: The chart below shows the number of cars bought by Millennials and Gen X buyers in a country from the year 2010 to 2020, as well as the yearly fuel consumption of the country (in Million liters). Considering the data presented in the chart, which one of the following options is true?

options.text: The percentage increase in fuel consumption from 2010 to 2020 is more than the percentage increase in fuel consumption from 2015 to 2020.
The increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is less than the decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020.
The decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is more than the decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2010 to 2015.
The decrease in the number of Gen X car buyers from 2015 to 2020 is more than the increase in the number of Millennial car buyers from 2015 to 2020.

sourceQuestion: Let G be a simple, finite, undirected graph with vertex set {v_1, ..., v_n}. Let Delta(G) denote the maximum degree of G and let N = {1, 2, ...} denote the set of all possible colors. Color the vertices of G using the following greedy strategy: for i = 1, ..., n: color(v_i) <- min{j in N : no neighbour of v_i is colored j}. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE? A. This procedure results in a proper vertex coloring of G.  B. The number of colors used is at most Delta(G)+1.  C. The number of colors used is at most Delta(G).  D. The number of colors used is equal to the chromatic number of G.

parsed.stem: Let G be a simple, finite, undirected graph with vertex set {v_1, ..., v_n}. Let Delta(G) denote the maximum degree of G and let N = {1, 2, ...} denote the set of all possible colors. Color the vertices of G using the following greedy strategy: for i = 1, ..., n: color(v_i) <- min{j in N : no neighbour of v_i is colored j}. Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

options.text: This procedure results in a proper vertex coloring of G.
The number of colors used is at most Delta(G)+1.
The number of colors used is at most Delta(G).
The number of colors used is equal to the chromatic number of G.

9.8.4 Bar Graph: GATE CSE 2021 Set 2 | GA Question: 6

The number of units of a product sold in three different years and the respective net profits are presented in the figure above. The cost/unit in Year 3 was F, which was half the cost/unit in Year 2. The cost/unit in Year 3 was one-third of the cost/unit in Year 1. Taxes were paid on the selling price at 30%, 10%, and 10% respectively for the three years. Net profit is calculated as the difference between the selling price and the sum of cost and taxes.

The ratio of the selling price in Year 2 to the selling price in Year 3 is:

A. 4 : 3
B. 1 : 1
C. 3 : 4
D. 1 : 2

sourceQuestion:
The chart given below compares the Installed Capacity (MW) of four power generation technologies, T1, T2, T3, and T4, and their Electricity Generation (MWh) in a time of 1000 hours (h).

The Capacity Factor of a power generation technology is:

Capacity Factor = Electricity Generation (MWh) / (Installed Capacity (MW) x 1000 h)

Which one of the given technologies has the highest Capacity Factor?

A. T1    B. T2    C. T3    D. T4

parsed.stem:
The chart given below compares the Installed Capacity (MW) of four power generation technologies, T1, T2, T3, and T4, and their Electricity Generation (MWh) in a time of 1000 hours (h).

The Capacity Factor of a power generation technology is:

Capacity Factor = Electricity Generation (MWh) / (Installed Capacity (MW) x 1000 h)

Which one of the given technologies has the highest Capacity Factor?

sourceQuestion:
The number of patients per shift (X) consulting Dr. Gita in her past 100 shifts is shown in the figure. If the amount she earns is Rs. 1000(X - 0.2) per shift (where X is the number of patients in that shift), what is the average amount (in Rs.) she has earned per shift in the past 100 shifts?

Note: The figure shown is representative.

A. 6,100    B. 6,300    C. 6,000    D. 6,500

Note: The figure shown is representative.

sourceQuestion:

[Bar graph: Grouped bars for each year from 2014 to 2018. School P values (in hundreds): 2014=450, 2015=350, 2016=500, 2017=400, 2018=300. School Q values (in hundreds): 2014=200, 2015=150, 2016=250, 2017=200, 2018=150.]

A. 5 : 23  B. 23 : 5  C. 25 : 32  D. 33 : 23

sourceQuestion:
The bar chart shows the data for the percentage of population falling into different categories based on Body Mass Index (BMI) in 2009 and 2023. Based on the data provided, which one of the following options is INCORRECT?

A. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into overweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has increased in 20 years.
B. The percentage of population falling into underweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.
C. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into obese category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.
D. The percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.
sourceAnswer: C. The ratio of the percentage of population falling into obese category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.

parsed.stem: The bar chart shows the data for the percentage of population falling into different categories based on Body Mass Index (BMI) in 2009 and 2023. Based on the data provided, which one of the following options is INCORRECT?

options.text: The ratio of the percentage of population falling into overweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has increased in 20 years.
The percentage of population falling into underweight category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.
The ratio of the percentage of population falling into obese category to the percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.
The percentage of population falling into normal category has decreased in 20 years.

sourceQuestion:
The revenue and expenditure of four different companies P, Q, R and S in 2015 are shown in the figure. If the revenue of company Q in 2013 was 20% more than that in 2015, and company Q had earned a profit of 10% on expenditure in 2013, then its expenditure (in million rupees) in 2013 was _______.

A. 32.7
B. 33.7
C. 34.1
D. 35.1

parsed.stem: The revenue and expenditure of four different companies P, Q, R and S in 2015 are shown in the figure. If the revenue of company Q in 2013 was 20% more than that in 2015, and company Q had earned a profit of 10% on expenditure in 2013, then its expenditure (in million rupees) in 2013 was _______.

sourceQuestion:
The bar chart gives the batting averages of YK and BS for 11 calendar years from 2012 to 2022. Considering that 2013 and 2019 are world cup years, which one of the following options is true?

A. BS has a higher yearly batting average than that of YK in every world cup year.
B. YK has a higher yearly batting average than that of BS in every world cup year.
C. YK's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of BS in every world cup year.
D. BS's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of YK in the last three years.
sourceAnswer: D. BS's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of YK in the last three years.

parsed.stem: The bar chart gives the batting averages of YK and BS for 11 calendar years from 2012 to 2022. Considering that 2013 and 2019 are world cup years, which one of the following options is true?

options.text: BS has a higher yearly batting average than that of YK in every world cup year.
YK has a higher yearly batting average than that of BS in every world cup year.
YK's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of BS in every world cup year.
BS's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of YK in the last three years.

sourceQuestion:
The bar graph shows the data of the students who appeared and passed in an examination for four schools P, Q, R, and S. The average of success rates (as percentage) of these four schools is ______.

Bar chart data (approximate from graph):
- School P: Appeared = 200, Passed = 100
- School Q: Appeared = 250, Passed = 150
- School R: Appeared = 300, Passed = 200
- School S: Appeared = 200, Passed = 100

A. 56.5%  B. 58.8%  C. 59.6%  D. 58.3%

- School Q: Appeared = 250, Passed = 150

- School S: Appeared = 200, Passed = 100

sourceQuestion:
The bar chart gives the batting averages of VK and RS for 11 calendar years from 2012 to 2022. Considering that 2015 and 2019 are world cup years, which one of the following options is true?

A. RS has a higher yearly batting average than that of VK in every world cup year.
B. VK has a higher yearly batting average than that of RS in every world cup year.
C. VK's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of RS in both the two world cup years.
D. RS's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of VK in the last three years.
sourceAnswer: C. VK's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of RS in both the two world cup years.

parsed.stem: The bar chart gives the batting averages of VK and RS for 11 calendar years from 2012 to 2022. Considering that 2015 and 2019 are world cup years, which one of the following options is true?

options.text: RS has a higher yearly batting average than that of VK in every world cup year.
VK has a higher yearly batting average than that of RS in every world cup year.
VK's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of RS in both the two world cup years.
RS's yearly batting average is consistently higher than that of VK in the last three years.

sourceQuestion:
The exports and imports (in crores of Rs.) of a country from 2000 to 2007 are given in the following bar chart. If the trade deficit is defined as excess of imports over exports, in which year is the trade deficit 1/5th of the exports?

A. 2005   B. 2004   C. 2007   D. 2006

parsed.stem: The exports and imports (in crores of Rs.) of a country from 2000 to 2007 are given in the following bar chart. If the trade deficit is defined as excess of imports over exports, in which year is the trade deficit 1/5th of the exports?

sourceQuestion:
The bar graph below shows the output of five carpenters over one month, each of whom made different items of furniture: chairs, tables, and beds.

Consider the following statements:
i. The number of beds made by carpenter C2 is exactly the same as the number of tables made by carpenter C3.
ii. The total number of chairs made by all carpenters is less than the total number of tables.

Which one of the following is true?

A. Only i   B. Only ii   C. Both i and ii   D. Neither i nor ii

parsed.stem:
The bar graph below shows the output of five carpenters over one month, each of whom made different items of furniture: chairs, tables, and beds.

Consider the following statements:
i. The number of beds made by carpenter C2 is exactly the same as the number of tables made by carpenter C3.
ii. The total number of chairs made by all carpenters is less than the total number of tables.

Which one of the following is true?

sourceQuestion:
The bar graph in panel (a) shows the proportion of male and female illiterates in 2001 and 2011. The proportions of males and females in 2001 and 2011 are given in Panels (b) and (c), respectively. The total population did not change during this period. The percentage increase in the total number of literates from 2001 to 2011 is ___.

A. 30.43   B. 33.43   C. 31.43   D. 35.43

A. 2011  B. 2012  C. 2013  D. 2014

sourceQuestion:
A person was born on the fifth Monday of February in a particular year. Which one of the following statements is correct based on the above information?

A. The 2nd February of that year is a Tuesday
B. There will be five Sundays in the month of February in that year
C. The 1st February of that year is a Sunday
D. All Mondays of February in that year have even dates
sourceAnswer: A. The 2nd February of that year is a Tuesday

parsed.stem: A person was born on the fifth Monday of February in a particular year. Which one of the following statements is correct based on the above information?

options.text: The 2nd February of that year is a Tuesday
There will be five Sundays in the month of February in that year
The 1st February of that year is a Sunday
All Mondays of February in that year have even dates
